title: Scopolia lanceolata
intro: Scopolia lanceolata, also known as Devil's Breath or Angel's Trumpet, is a perennial plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Solanaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Scopolia lanceolata include tropane alkaloids, such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological activity. In herbal medicine, Scopolia lanceolata is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including restlessness, agitation, and muscle spasms.
taxonomy: Scopolia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the nightshade family. It is native to South Africa. The plant belongs to the genus Scopolia. The family Solanaceae is part of the larger group known as the asterids within the clade angiosperms. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

side_effects: Scopolia lanceolata is known to be a plant with potential side effects due to its alkaloid content, particularly scopolamine and hyoscyamine. These compounds can cause various adverse reactions, including dry mouth, blurred vision, and increased heart rate. In some cases, high doses of scopolia lanceolata may lead to more severe side effects, such as hallucinations, delirium, and respiratory depression. Furthermore, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using this plant due to its potential teratogenic and abortifacient effects. Prolonged use of scopolia lanceolata can also lead to physical dependence and withdrawal symptoms upon cessation.
